title: Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste — a beloved Korean classic

description: Doenjang Jjigae is a quintessential Korean comfort food, a hearty and flavorful soybean paste stew cherished by many. This bubbling hotpot is packed with a rich umami depth from fermented soybean paste, combined with an array of fresh vegetables and often tofu or meat. It is a truly satisfying and wholesome dish perfect for any meal, especially with a bowl of steamed rice.

instructions.title: Prepare the Anchovy Broth
instructions.text: In a pot, combine 5 cups of water with dried anchovies and dried kelp. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes. Remove the anchovies and kelp, reserving the flavorful broth. This dashi-like broth forms the base of your jjigae.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables and Aromatics
instructions.text: While the broth simmers, prepare your vegetables. Dice the tofu, slice the zucchini into half-moons, dice the potato, and slice the onion. Mince the garlic and chop the green onions. If using, slice the green chili pepper and thinly slice the beef or pork.

instructions.title: Combine Base Ingredients
instructions.text: In a ttukbaegi (Korean earthenware pot) or a medium pot, add the prepared anchovy broth. Stir in the doenjang until it dissolves completely. If using, add the gochujang for a spicier kick. Add the diced potato, sliced onion, and minced garlic to the pot.

instructions.title: Simmer the Stew
instructions.text: Bring the stew to a boil over medium-high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low, cover, and let it simmer for about 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are almost tender. If adding meat, add it now and cook until no longer pink.

instructions.title: Add Remaining Vegetables and Tofu
instructions.text: Add the sliced zucchini, mushrooms, and diced firm tofu to the simmering stew. Continue to cook for another 5-7 minutes, or until the zucchini is tender-crisp and the tofu is heated through. The flavors should be well-integrated.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the chopped green onions and sliced green chili pepper (if using) just before serving. Let it simmer for one more minute. Serve the bubbling hot Doenjang Jjigae immediately with freshly steamed white rice and various banchan (side dishes).

tips: For a deeper, richer flavor, gently pan-fry the thinly sliced beef or pork belly with a little sesame oil and garlic before adding it to the stew base.
Adjust the amount of doenjang and gochujang to your personal taste. Some doenjang brands are saltier than others, so taste as you go.
Doenjang Jjigae is best served bubbling hot directly from the pot (ttukbaegi) it was cooked in. It pairs perfectly with plain white rice and any Korean side dishes.
article: Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste is a beloved Korean Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
